steps.m: to praise, glorify (by declaring something free of faults)

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra effort or repeatedly
Changed from past tense (سَبَّحَ) to present tense (يُسَبِّحُ) by adding يُ at the start. The ُ ending shows this is happening now.
This is the base word - Allah's proper name. It's always written with shadda on the ل
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is being done for or to Allah. The first ا is removed when لِ is added. This word is receiving the action of يُسَبِّحُ (glorifies/declares perfection)
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this word is being affected by something else in the sentence
This is a relative pronoun that refers to things rather than people. In this ayah, it refers to everything in the heavens that glorifies Allah.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This preposition shows where the glorifying (يُسَبِّحُ) happens - in the heavens.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun for the place that is high up
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Added 'the' at the beginning, which makes the س double (shown by ّ)

steps.m: the heavens (possessed by/connected to something after it)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it, and changed long a (ا) to ٰ
This is a relative pronoun that refers to things (not people). Here it refers to everything in the earth.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word connects to 'in the earth' (في الأرض) that comes after it.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows what exists within the heavens.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fi'
Changed the ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. Both are pronounced the same way.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a concrete noun referring to the earth or ground
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on/at) in the ayah
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'one who rules'
Makes the word specific by adding 'the' to it
Shows this word is being described or possessed by something
Root 'holy' turned into special form with double middle letter (shadda) and u-sounds to mean 'extremely holy' - like Allah's perfect holiness
The ال makes this a specific title of Allah, showing this is His unique attribute

steps.m: The Intensely Holy One (describing اللَّهِ, following الْمَلِكِ)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word follows and describes الْمَلِكِ (The King) as another title of Allah
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he was mighty' in the past tense
Changed pattern to show this is a permanent quality
Added ال to make it definite, referring specifically to Allah in this context
Added kasra at the end to show this is describing Allah in the genitive case
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who has this quality strongly
Added ال to make it definite and ِ to show it describes Allah. This is one of Allah's names describing His perfect wisdom.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that points to someone. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is the one that sent a messenger.
This is the basic form that points to someone or something we're about to describe
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
The ل gets a shadda (double letter mark) and the first letter changes to ٱ to help with pronunciation. This word connects to بَعَثَ (sent/raised) to tell us who did the sending.

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to يَتْلُوا۟ (recites) in the ayah, showing who is doing the reciting.
This is the basic preposition that shows location or position

steps.m: in (connects to ٱلْأُمِّيِّۦنَ - among the unlettered people)
steps.n: The ى is added at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. In this ayah, it shows that the messenger was sent specifically among or within the unlettered people.
Added damma and shadda to create the basic noun form

steps.m: unlettered (one who remains in original/natural state)
steps.n: Adds relational suffix يّ (yaa with shadda) to create attribution to أمّ (mother/source/origin), meaning 'one who is in original/natural state' or 'unlettered'
Adds sound masculine plural ending ين to create plural form
Makes the word definite

steps.m: the unlettered ones (object of بَعَثَ - sent)
steps.n: Added accusative case ending to show this word is the object of بَعَثَ (sent). Also added the small ya (ۦ) for proper recitation.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role

steps.m: a messenger (object of بَعَثَ - He sent)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of being sent. This connects to the verb بَعَثَ (He sent) in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added emphasis on the م with a shadda for pronunciation after joining with previous word
Added sukun on ن to show end of first word

steps.m: from them (refers to الْأُمِّيِّۦنَ - the unlettered people)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (referring back to the unlettered people mentioned earlier)

steps.m: to follow closely or recite carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to sukun, and final vowel to damma
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show 'them' (referring to الْأُمِّيِّۦنَ - the unlettered people mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: to show a sign or mark

steps.n: The ة at the end shows this is a single sign/verse
The words are actually the same - just written with slightly different spelling. Both mean a single sign/verse/miracle.
Changes singular (one verse) to plural (many verses) by adding ٰت
Adds a small i-sound at the end to show these verses belong to something
Adds هِ meaning 'His' to show these are Allah's verses. This word is what is being recited in the verb يَتْلُوا (he recites).
The shadda makes the action stronger - like purifying something thoroughly
The ي at the start shows it's happening now (present tense)
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous actions of the messenger: reciting (يَتْلُوا) and teaching (يُعَلِّمُ)
Added attached pronoun هِمْ (them) as direct object
Added shadda (double mark) on middle letter to show intensive teaching
Adds يُ prefix to show present tense (not passive - it's still active voice)
Adds 'them' (هُمُ) to the end of 'he teaches'
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha marks
The verb 'he wrote' changes to mean 'book' - like how writing creates a book. The word كِتٰب shows what is being taught.

steps.m: the book (object being taught in يُعَلِّمُ)
steps.n: The word gets 'the' (ال) at the start and a small 'a' sound (-َ) at the end since it's receiving the action of teaching
Changes the action into a concept by adding specific vowel patterns
Adds ة to show one specific instance or manifestation of wisdom
Adds ال to make it specific and definite
Adds و to connect this word to what came before it (the Book)
Adds fatha at the end to show this is the object being taught in the phrase 'teaching them the Book and wisdom'
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Basic preposition showing that the messenger (رَسُولًا) comes from among them (هُمْ). In this ayah, it shows that Allah chose a messenger from within the community He was sending the message to.

steps.m: to be in front of something

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'before'
Adds damma to show this word is in the nominative case
Combination of emphatic 'surely' (لَ) and 'in' (فِى)

steps.m: to lose the way, to go off track

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changes from a verb (action) to a noun (concept)
Changed to ضَلٰلٍ with tanween kasra (-in sound) because it comes after the word في (in) which needs this ending
The مُ prefix with the middle letter kasra (ِ) creates an active participle - something or someone that makes things clear
The two dots under the last letter (ٍ) make it indefinite and show it's being used as an object
The double letter (ّ) makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Basic root meaning of coming after something else
Changed from basic root letters to an adjective meaning 'later' by adding the short vowels a-a
Added long alif at start to make it mean 'another/other' instead of just 'later'
Added ين at the end to make it plural, meaning multiple others

steps.m: and others (connected to previous group mentioned)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this group with the previously mentioned group
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from them (referring to others who will join later)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to other people who will join the community later, as mentioned in وَءَاخَرِينَ (and others)
Starting with لَ which gives emphasis

steps.m: not yet (referring to يَلْحَقُوا۟ - they have joined)
steps.n: Adding مَّا changes the meaning to indicate something hasn't happened yet. In this ayah, it refers to others who haven't yet joined the first group.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb connects to بِهِمْ (with them) in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or what it's connected to
Added هِ to show this applies to him/it

steps.m: with them (refers to others who haven't joined yet)
steps.n: Changed from singular (him) to plural (them) by adding م. This refers back to the 'others' (ءَاخَرِينَ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the basic pronoun meaning 'he'
Added conjunction وَ at beginning to connect this word to what follows it. This shows that 'he' (Allah) is also 'the Mighty, the Wise' (ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ)
Basic meaning of being mighty or powerful
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed pattern to فَعِيل to show someone/something that has this quality strongly

steps.m: The Mighty One (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's a subject. This is one of Allah's names, showing His perfect might and power.

steps.m: to judge wisely, to make wise decisions
steps.n: Basic meaning of wisdom and judgment
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show someone who has the quality of wisdom

steps.m: The wise, the one full of wisdom
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The first letter gets a special mark (ٱ) to help with pronunciation when connecting words.

steps.m: The All-Wise (one of Allah's names, paired with العزيز/The Mighty)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this describes Allah in the sentence. It pairs with العزيز (The Mighty) to describe Allah's perfect wisdom and power.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for pointing to something nearby. It's a fundamental word that doesn't come from a root.
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show elongation, which helps indicate distance.
Adding ل with a kasra (ِ) changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far).

steps.m: that (points to something far) (subject of فَضْلُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word and makes it specifically masculine. This word is the subject that فَضْلُ (bounty) is describing in this ayah.

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to make it into a noun. This word refers to Allah's special gifts or favors that He gives.
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) in the sentence. It's the subject of the verb يُؤْتِيهِ (He gives it).
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the name for God in Arabic.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل. This word shows who the favor (فَضْلُ) belongs to in this ayah.
Connected the root letters together to form the base word
Added يُ at start to show present tense and 'he' as subject. The hamza moved after the ي with sukun (ؤْ) due to Arabic spelling rules.

steps.m: he gives it (object of فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added هِ at end to show what receives the action. This word refers to Allah's bounty (فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَشَآءُ (He wills/wants) to mean 'whoever He wills/wants'.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Adding يَ at start changes it from past to present tense
Adding ُ at the end shows this is in the normal present tense form
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يُؤْتِيهِ - gives)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (giving) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يُؤْتِيهِ - gives)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement, and added ٱ (alif with wasl) which helps pronunciation when connecting words
This is a special word that shows someone or something has or owns something. It's like saying 'has' or 'with' in English.
Added و at the end to show this word is describing Allah in this ayah. It shows that Allah has or possesses something (in this case, great favor/bounty).

steps.m: to give extra, to favor, to be generous

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'favor' by adding specific vowel marks
The prefix ٱلْ makes the noun definite, while the kasra ending ِ indicates the genitive case, showing this noun belongs to or follows another word
Added ي between ظ and م to show this describes something with a strong quality. This word describes Allah's bounty (فَضْلِ) in this ayah.
Adding ال makes it specific/definite - now referring to a particular magnificent thing
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to the word before it (فَضْلِ)
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and create a noun meaning 'example'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of بِئْسَ (how evil/bad is) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ٱلَّ at the beginning makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: those who (describes the people who rejected Allah's signs)
steps.n: Adding ينَ at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person. In this ayah, it refers to the people who rejected Allah's signs.

steps.m: was made to carry (passive form of carry)
steps.n: Changed the word to show something was done to someone rather than someone doing it themselves. The ُ (damma) on first letter and ِ (kasra) with ّ (shadda) on middle letter show this passive meaning.

steps.m: they were made to carry (referring to those who were given Torah)
steps.n: The وا at the end shows this happened to a group of people (in this case, those who were given the Torah)
This is the base word for the Jewish holy book, derived from a root meaning light or guidance
Adding shadda emphasizes the word's importance
Added 'the' at the beginning and changed the alif to an extended alif
Added fatha to show this is the object of the verb 'were given to carry/bear' (حُمِّلُوا)

steps.m: then (connects to لَمْ يَحْمِلُوهَا - they did not carry it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects to the next part of the ayah about not carrying the Torah.

steps.m: not (negates يَحْمِلُوهَا - they did not carry it)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَحْمِلُوهَا) to show it didn't happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
The يَ at the beginning shows this is happening in the present, and the ِ (kasra) on مِ shows it's active voice
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is in the nominative case
The و at the end shows multiple people (men) are doing the action

steps.m: they carry it (referring to التَّوْرَىٰةَ - the Torah)
steps.n: Adding هَا shows what is being carried (the Torah mentioned earlier in the verse)
Changed from basic root 'to compare' into noun form by adding vowels (fat-ha marks) to make the word mean 'example'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is affected by a preposition or possession
Added كَ at the beginning to compare this to something else. In this ayah, it's comparing to 'the donkey' (الْحِمَارِ) that comes after it.
This pattern فِعال is commonly used for animal names
The ال makes it definite (the), and the kasra ending shows it's in the genitive case
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: he carries/bears (the subject of لَمْ يَحْمِلُوهَا - they did not carry it)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, kasra on middle root letter, and damma at end. In this ayah, this word is part of لَمْ يَحْمِلُوهَا (they did not carry it), referring to those who were given the Torah but didn't uphold it.
Books are called 'سِفْر' because they reveal and spread knowledge, like a journey spreads people across distances
This form makes the word plural, showing we're talking about multiple books
The ًۢا ending shows these are books (the direct object) - meaning something is being done to them
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a word used to criticize or condemn something
Added fathas to create a noun meaning 'example' or 'comparison'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to stand firmly, to rise up, to establish

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ because it's the object of يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah
Starting with the basic demonstrative particle and adding ي to make it a relative pronoun
Adding ال with shadda on ذ makes it more specific and definite
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people
Doubling the middle letter ذ makes the meaning stronger and more intense

steps.m: they strongly denied/rejected (the signs of Allah)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show a group of people (they) did this action. This word connects to بِـَٔايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ (the signs of Allah) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة at the end makes this a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Makes the word plural by adding ات
Adds بِ meaning 'with/by' at start and ِ at end because it's connected to the next word (اللَّهِ)
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word used so often
Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at end to show this connects to the word بِـَٔايٰتِ (signs/verses) before it, meaning 'signs/verses of Allah'
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. The word comes from the root meaning 'deity' but has become a specific name.
The shadda shows that the letter ل is doubled in pronunciation.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of لَا يَهْدِى/does not guide)
steps.n: The damma at the end shows that Allah is the subject (doer) of the action in this sentence - specifically the one who 'does not guide' the wrongdoing people.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of لَا يَهْدِى/does not guide)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this part to what came before it in the verse, like saying 'and'. The small alif (ٱ) helps with pronunciation.
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen. Here it negates the verb يَهْدِى (guides), making it mean 'does not guide'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he guided' by adding vowels
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word shows Allah guiding the people in the ayah.

steps.m: people (as a group that stands together)
steps.n: Changed from an action to a noun meaning the group that does that action. The fatha and sukun pattern creates a noun from the root.
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular people, and َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of being described in an example
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who wrongs others (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show this describes someone who does the action. This word is affected by the verb يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah.

steps.m: those who wrong others (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural (more than two). The ِ vowel shows this word receives the action of the verb يَهْدِى (guides).

steps.m: the wrongdoers (who Allah does not guide)
steps.n: Added ٱل (the) at start to make it specific. The َ at the end shows this receives the action of not being guided. The ّ (shadda) appears because ل from ٱل combines with ظ.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form. The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter, and we add damma and sukun to show it's a command. This is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say something to others.
This is a calling phrase used to get attention, like saying 'Hey you!' or 'O you!'
Changed from root letters to a simple sound used to call out to someone
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to emphasize the call
Added fatha mark to show proper pronunciation
Added the vocative particle يا to start calling someone
The doubled letter and damma make the word stronger when calling someone
Adding ها makes it clear we're calling a specific person or group
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'a' sounds
Added plural past tense ending وا to show multiple people did this action
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if you are' (كُنتُمْ) with what comes before it, meaning 'if you are truthful, then...'

steps.m: to claim falsely or without proof

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for suffix attachment
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past

This is an emphasis particle that connects what comes before with what comes after, making the second part dependent on the first. Here it connects with زَعَمْتُمْ (you claimed) to show what was claimed.

steps.m: that you all (object of زَعَمْتُمْ - you claimed)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمْ to show who the statement is about. This connects to زَعَمْتُمْ (you claimed) to show what was claimed.

steps.m: to protect, to be close to and support

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect/support'
Changed from single 'protector' to plural 'protectors' using special pattern for important roles
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject of the sentence
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added لِ at beginning to show direction or purpose. In this ayah, it shows belonging exclusively to Allah.

steps.m: from (connects دُونِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects دُونِ (besides/other than) to the previous part of the sentence.
This is a particle that indicates something being beneath or lower in status
Adding the damma (ُ) vowel mark changes how we say the word
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to الناس (the people) that comes after it, showing it means 'besides/other than the people'

steps.m: to be social, familiar with others
steps.n: This is from the root ن-و-س but is treated as a standalone word
Adding alif after ن changes it to mean 'those who are social/familiar'
Adding ال makes it specific/definite - 'the people' instead of just 'people'
The ِ at the end shows this word comes after دُونِ (besides) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the wishing is done with intensity or multiple times
Adding تَ at start shows the wishing is being done to/for oneself
Adding وا makes it plural - now means multiple people wished
Added فَ (meaning 'so/then') at start + changed from past tense to command form
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The و gets a sukun (ْ) because it's a weak letter.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ا in ال is written as ٱ when starting a word.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's the object of the verb فَتَمَنَّوُا۟ (then wish for).

steps.m: if (introduces condition for زَعَمْتُمْ - you claim)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if you claim' with what comes after it, showing that something depends on their claim.
This root represents the basic concept of being or existing
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form
The middle weak letter و drops out, and the first vowel changes to damma to compensate
Added تُمْ to show this is talking about multiple people (you all) in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter and kasra after second letter to create active participle pattern that describes someone who does the action
Added ين to make the word refer to multiple truthful people (masculine plural)
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.m: and not (they will not wish for it ever)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This particle connects the negative statement about not wishing for death with previous statements.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added Form V pattern markers: تَ prefix and shadda on middle root letter to show intensive meaning of wishing
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start
Added plural masculine ending

steps.m: they wish for it (negated by لا)
steps.n: Added object pronoun 'it'. The word is negated by لا before it, so the full phrase means 'they do not wish for it'
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to make it a noun meaning endless time

steps.m: forever (describes how long they will not wish for it)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it describe how the action happens. This word tells us that the action of يَتَمَنَّوْنَ (they wish) will never happen, not even once.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added the preposition بِ to show cause/reason. This word connects to قَدَّمَتْ (they sent ahead) to explain why something happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle root letter indicates intensification of the basic meaning of preceding/putting forward

steps.m: she sent ahead (referring to what أَيْدِيهِمْ/their hands did)
steps.n: Added feminine past tense marker ت to show the subject is feminine (their hands/أَيْدِيهِمْ, which is feminine in Arabic)
Basic meaning of the root
Root meaning 'power/control' becomes the word for 'hand' - like how we say 'give me a hand' to mean 'help/power'
Changes 'hand' to 'hands' using a special plural pattern with أ at start and ي in middle
Adds kasra at end to complete the plural form

steps.m: their hands (referring to those who did wrong in the previous ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'their' when talking about a group. Here it refers back to those mentioned in the previous ayah who did wrong.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added 'L' sound (shadda) to show this is not just any god, but the one true God - Allah
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in عَلِيمٌ (All-Knowing)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Basic meaning of the root
Changes from basic verb to intensive form meaning 'one who knows deeply'

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm others
steps.n: Basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show someone who does this action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ل combines with ظ to make it stronger (shown by ّ)

steps.m: with/by the people who act unfairly
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show these people are being affected by something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the weak middle letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows. It's like adding an exclamation mark or saying 'Listen up!' before an important statement. Here it makes the command 'say' stronger.
Added vowels to turn the action of dying into the noun 'death'. The و with sukun (ْ) makes a long 'o' sound.
Added ال at beginning to make it specific ('the death'). The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is affected by إِنَّ (indeed) which comes before it in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they describe

steps.m: the one who/which (referring to death in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific. In this ayah, it refers back to 'death' (الموت) that was just mentioned.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with a shadda
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تَ at start and changing first vowel to kasra
Added ون to show multiple people are running away
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what we're moving away from. In this ayah, it refers back to death (ٱلْمَوْتَ) that was mentioned earlier.
This is the basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement

steps.m: then indeed it/he (referring to الْمَوْتَ - death)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to refer back to death (الْمَوْتَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The small ۥ mark is just to show the vowel is long.
Adding vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Added مُ at start and ا after ق to make it mean 'one who meets' instead of just 'met'

steps.m: the one who will meet you all (referring to death in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to multiple people being spoken to. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.

This word connects two events in sequence, showing that being returned (تُرَدُّونَ) happens after meeting death. It's like saying 'first this happens, then that happens' in English. The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger to emphasize the sequence.

steps.m: to send back or return something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
When two same letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show someone is doing this to you (passive) in present time
Added ون at the end to show this refers to a group of people. This word connects to عٰلِمِ (Knower) in the ayah, showing who they are being returned to.
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to عٰلِمِ (Knower) to show you are being returned to the All-Knowing.
Added small alif (ٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added alif after first letter and changed middle vowel to kasra to make it mean 'one who does the action'
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ). The kasra at the end shows this word owns or possesses what comes after it (الغيب - the unseen).
Changes from an action (being hidden) to a noun (a hidden thing). The vowel pattern َ_ْ_ turns verbs into nouns.
Added ال to make it specific ('the unseen' not just any 'unseen thing'). The ِ at the end shows it belongs to عٰلِمِ (Knower), forming 'Knower of the unseen'.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changes verb into noun showing the act of witnessing
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and doubles the ش sound
Adds conjunction to connect with previous word (الغيب - the unseen)

steps.m: and the witnessed (possessed by عٰلِمِ - Knower of)
steps.n: Changes to 'the witnessed' because it's owned by 'Knower of' (like saying 'Knower of the witnessed')
This word means 'witnessing/testimony' and is connected to the word before it (like saying 'of the witnessing')

steps.m: to inform or tell important news

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens after something else

steps.m: then he will inform you all
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the action
Basic preposition that shows how something is done
Added مَا which means 'what' to specify what the preposition refers to. In this ayah, it connects to تَعْمَلُونَ (you used to do) to mean 'with what you used to do'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
The word is shortened to give a command - 'be!' instead of 'he was', dropping the middle و
Added تُمْ to show this is about multiple people in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you (all) do/work (referring to the disbelievers being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This refers to the disbelievers who are trying to flee from death in this ayah.

steps.m: to point to or direct attention
steps.n: This is a calling word used to get someone's attention
The word يا (O!) was removed from the beginning, leaving just أيّ (which/who)
Added the calling particle يٰٓ to make it into a direct address
Added ها to make it a complete calling phrase
Starting with the basic demonstrative word 'this' and changing it to mean 'who/which'
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly - shadda on ل shows it's pronounced strongly
Adding ن at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word describes the people who believed (ءَامَنُوٓا۟) in this verse.
Adding 'hamza' at the start makes it mean 'to make something happen' - in this case, to make trust happen means to believe
Adding long 'aa' sound after first letter makes it mean 'to do something with someone else'
Added وا at the end to show a group of people (3 or more) did the action
This is the base form of the word that indicates a specific point in time

Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future events and creates a condition - when this happens, then something else will happen. In this ayah, it means 'when the call to prayer is made' (نُودِىَ لِلصَّلَوٰةِ)
This root refers to the action of calling out or announcing something
Added the vowels 'a' to make it a simple past tense verb meaning 'he called out'
Added و to mark this as a passive past tense verb
The و changes to ة to make it a noun meaning 'prayer'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to pray' into the noun 'prayer' by replacing و with ا and adding ة at the end
Added shadda to emphasize the importance of prayer
Added لِل which combines لِ (for/to) with ال (the)
The alif (ا) became a waw with a small alif on top (وٰ), and got a kasra (ِ) ending because it follows the word 'for' (لِ)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from. In this ayah, it connects 'the day' (يَوْمِ) to 'when it is called' (نُودِىَ), showing when the call starts.
This root represents the concept of a day or period of time
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. The middle letter و gets a sukoon (ْ) to keep it as a consonant.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it (الْجُمُعَةِ meaning Friday). Together they mean 'of Friday'.
This pattern with ة makes it a noun for the day when people gather
Added damma vowels to create a noun meaning the concept of gathering
Added ة to specify one particular instance - here referring to the weekly gathering day (Friday)
Added ال to make it definite, referring to the specific weekly gathering day
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is possessed by يَوْمِ (day), forming 'day of Friday'

steps.m: to move quickly towards a goal

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels. The ي changes to ى at the end because that's how past tense verbs end.
Changed from 'he moved quickly' to a command by adding اِ at start and dropping the ى at end - like saying 'run!' to someone
Added وْا to make it a command for a group

steps.m: so move quickly! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with what came before it. The ٱ is just a different way to write اِ.
Added kasra (ِ) under alif to show this is a preposition showing direction
Added fatha (َ) on lam to help with pronunciation
The alif maksura (ىٰ) is actually part of the original word إلى - the small alif above just shows proper pronunciation. It points towards Allah's remembrance (ذِكْرِ) in this ayah
Changed the verb into a noun by adding the vowels ِ and ْ. This makes it mean the act itself rather than doing it.
Added ِ at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes right after it, forming the phrase 'remembrance of Allah'
Added ال and merged it with the word, changing إ to ل to create the unique name Allah
Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to ٱ for connection with previous word, and added kasra at end to show possession
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added damma at the end to show it's in the nominative case
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people

steps.m: and leave! (all of you) (connected to فَٱسْعَوْا۟ - then rush)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous command 'then rush' (فَٱسْعَوْا۟)
Changed the root letters into a noun pattern to show the action itself rather than doing the action. This word is what's being acted on by the command 'leave' (ذَرُوا) in this ayah.
The ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English), and the ending vowel shows it's the object
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show this points to something far away instead of nearby
Added ل with kasra (ِ) which helps connect to suffixes that will be added

steps.m: that (pointing to something) for all of you
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this relates to multiple people being addressed
Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'good'
Added double-damma to show this word is doing the describing in the sentence
This is a basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to' that connects one word to another
Adding the shadda (doubling mark) on ل makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: surely for you all (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: The كم suffix shows this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the believers mentioned at the start with يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ (O you who believe)

This is a conditional particle that connects two parts: 1) the condition that follows 'if', and 2) what happens when that condition is true. Here it connects with كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (you know/understand) to mean 'if you know/understand'.
This is one of the most common roots in Arabic, used to show existence, being, or happening
Changed the root letters to make 'he was' by adding fathas (the small diagonal lines above)
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command meaning 'be!' by dropping the middle letter and changing vowels. Like saying 'do it!' instead of 'he did it'
Added تُ to show past tense first person (I)
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding تم at the end - a common way to make past tense verbs refer to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The ت at the start changes it from 'he knew' to 'you know', and the vowels show it's happening in the present
Adding ون at the end makes it plural - now talking to many people instead of one
This is a time word that introduces something happening at a specific time. Here it connects to when the prayer is completed.

steps.m: and/then when (connecting to قُضِيَتِ - is completed)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this is part of a sequence - after one thing happens (prayer), then something else should happen (spreading out). This connects the prayer being completed to what should happen next.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

steps.m: it (fem) was completed (referring to الصَّلَوٰةُ - the prayer)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت with kasra to match with الصَّلَوٰةُ (the prayer) which is feminine

steps.m: to pray or bow in worship

steps.n: Root letters of 'pray' connected with short vowels (fat-ha) to make basic word form
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a feminine noun referring to one specific instance of prayer

steps.m: the prayer (subject of قُضِيَتِ - was completed)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific, changed ا to وٰ for Quranic spelling, and added ُ to show it's the subject of 'was completed'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added اِن at beginning and ت after first letter to show doing action to oneself
Added فَ at beginning to show this happens after something else (after the prayer is completed)

steps.m: so spread yourselves out (command to group)
steps.n: Changed to command form for multiple people by adding وا at end and changing middle vowel to kasra

steps.m: in/within (shows location in الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: This is a helper word that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the spreading out happens in/on the earth.

steps.m: in/within (shows location in الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic noun pattern
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in)
Added ال to specify we're talking about THE earth, not just any land

steps.m: to seek or desire something actively

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make past tense. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing action thoroughly for oneself. The sukun (ْ) helps pronunciation.
Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group. The ى is dropped because of the ending.
Added وَ (and) at start. The ٱ is just a different way to write the initial alif.
This is a preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows they should seek from Allah's bounty.
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: to increase, exceed, or give extra
bounty, favor (something extra given as a gift)
steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create a noun meaning the result or gift that comes from giving extra. This word refers to Allah's special gifts to people.
Added the kasra (ِ) at the end to show this bounty belongs to Allah. This is like saying "Allah's bounty" in English.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or comes from Allah. This word is possessed by فَضْلِ (bounty) in the phrase 'bounty of Allah'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he remembered' to telling one person to remember (command form)
Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect with previous phrase
Changed the first letter to ل and added another ل with a shadda to make it the proper name for God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ - remember)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being remembered in the phrase 'remember Allah'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added ي to show this is a quality or characteristic

steps.m: much (describing how Allah should be remembered)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action (remembering Allah) should be done
Adding shadda on first letter makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: so that you all (might) (connected to تُفْلِحُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show it's addressing multiple people. This word connects to تُفْلِحُونَ (you succeed) to mean 'so that you all might succeed'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a compound particle made up of 'wa' (and) + 'idha' (when)

steps.m: to see/perceive with eyes or mind
steps.n: The basic past tense form of the verb
Changed from basic root 'to see' into past tense 'he saw' by adding vowels and writing ى at the end
Added وْا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The ى changes to و because of this addition.
Added ۟ mark to show this is a group of people doing the action
Basic past tense form of the root
Changes from verb (to trade) into noun (trade/commerce)

steps.m: trade/commerce (as object of رَأَوْا - they saw)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ً) to show this is the object being seen in the phrase 'they saw trade'
This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects 'business' (تِجٰرَةً) with 'amusement' (لَهْوًا) to show they saw either one of these things.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he was playful' changes to the noun 'amusement' by removing the vowel after و. Since something is seeing this amusement, it becomes the object in the sentence.

steps.m: as a distraction/amusement (object of رَأَوْا - they saw)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of seeing in the sentence. It's like saying 'they saw some entertainment.'
Connected the root letters with vowels and added shadda to show strength
Adding اِنْ at the beginning shows the action happens by itself
Adding وا at the end shows many people did this action
Changed spelling to match Uthmani script - اِنْ becomes ٱن and added small alif after و
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: towards her/it (referring to تِجٰرَةً 'business transaction' or لَهْوًا 'diversion')
steps.n: Added ها which shows this is happening to something feminine (in this case referring back to either the business transaction or diversion mentioned earlier)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Transforms singular 'left' to plural 'they left' by adding و suffix
The suffix كَ shows that 'you' is the object being left. This refers to the Prophet being left standing while others went to trade, as shown in the next word قَآئِمًا (standing).
The prefix "وَ" is a conjunction meaning "and". It connects this part of the sentence to what came before.
Basic past tense form of the root
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from verb (to stand) to noun meaning 'one who stands'

steps.m: standing (describing state of تَرَكُوكَ/they left you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how they left the Prophet (standing). The آ is just a writing style for اا.
These are the three basic letters that give us the meaning of saying or speaking
Added vowels to connect the letters together to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form. The middle letter 'و' drops out because it's a weak letter, and we add ُ and ْ to show it's a command
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) in this ayah to ask about what is with Allah. In this context, it's referring to the reward that Allah has.
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Adding vowel marks to create a word meaning location or possession. In this ayah, it shows that something is 'with Allah' or 'in Allah's presence'.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging it with the word. The hamza is dropped and the first ل gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word tells us what خَيْرٌ (better/best) belongs to or comes from. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes in the middle of speech.
Basic past tense form of the root
Changes from verb pattern to noun pattern showing the quality of being good

steps.m: better/best (as the subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Adding the two dots on top (tanween) makes this word mean 'better/best' and shows it's doing the action in the sentence
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Added sukun to show where the word normally ends.
The 'n' sound merges with the 'l' of the next word (اللَّهْوِ), and gets a fatha to link them together
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. The fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter pattern turns the action into a thing.
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific, and added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the sound of ل
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it follows the word مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Added fatha at the end to help connect with the next word (التِّجٰرَةِ)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This preposition connects to the word التِّجٰرَةِ (the commerce) that comes after it.
Basic past tense form of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he traded) to a noun meaning the activity itself, like changing 'swim' to 'swimming'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of trading
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ت with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from). Also changed alif to alif waslah since it's the definite article ال.
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of خَيْرُ (best) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of خَيْرُ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of خَيْرُ)
steps.n: Changed initial alif to waṣla form since it comes after و
Basic past tense form of the root
Changed from verb pattern to noun pattern by adding sukoon on middle letter
Added damma to show this word is in the subject position
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes verb to active participle noun pattern فَاعِل describing someone who performs the action
Makes word plural masculine and shows it's in genitive case after مِن (of/from)
Adds definite article ال with sun letter doubling (shadda) on ر, and final ـَ (fatha) for proper pronunciation
